title: The Fight Before Christmas

jokes.text: Wrong holiday, stupid.
jokes.setup: Homer apparently struggling with Christmas decorations
jokes.punchline: Dismissive self-correction suggesting he was trying to set up for the wrong holiday
jokes.explanation: Simple character moment showing Homer's confusion, but lacks distinctive wit

jokes.text: And that last touchdown makes it 67 to 13.
jokes.setup: Homer claims to be watching an 'important' bowl game
jokes.punchline: The absurdly lopsided score reveals it's clearly not competitive or important
jokes.explanation: Good character-based humor showing Homer's misplaced priorities and self-deception

jokes.text: Marking a crime scene. To celebrate an ancient pagan ritual, this tree was cut down and tarted up like a dime-a-dance floozy.
jokes.setup: Lisa being asked what she's doing with the Christmas tree
jokes.punchline: Her overly dramatic and pretentious description of Christmas tree decoration
jokes.explanation: Classic Lisa character comedy with sophisticated vocabulary applied to mundane situation

jokes.text: You mean your gingerbread McMansion.
jokes.setup: Marge mentions her gingerbread house
jokes.punchline: Lisa's critique using contemporary architecture terminology
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that fits Lisa's character and commentary on excess

jokes.text: Three gingerbread workmen died making that.
jokes.setup: Lisa criticizing the gingerbread house
jokes.punchline: Marge treating broken gingerbread pieces as workplace casualties
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor with dark undertones, unexpected from Marge

jokes.text: You're gonna be okay. It's almost over. Now to take care of the witnesses.
jokes.setup: Lisa performing 'surgery' on gingerbread man
jokes.punchline: Escalating from medical procedure to mob hit terminology
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from innocent to sinister, well-structured progression

jokes.text: Yep. Come midnight, I'll be sitting here with cold cookies, warm milk, and hot lead.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Santa and Christmas spirit
jokes.punchline: Bart's plan escalates from waiting for Santa to armed confrontation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with nice parallel structure contrasting innocent and violent elements

jokes.text: I've asked that rosy-cheeked nog-hog for a dirt bike
jokes.setup: Bart's complaint about Santa
jokes.punchline: Creative insult 'nog-hog' combining Christmas imagery with derogatory term
jokes.explanation: Creative and memorable insult that perfectly captures Bart's irreverent attitude

jokes.text: I wanted them till I got them.
jokes.setup: Bart being told he wanted those toys
jokes.punchline: Philosophical observation about desire and satisfaction
jokes.explanation: Surprisingly wise observation from Bart about consumer culture and human nature

jokes.text: Even Mac Davis himself couldn't save this Christmas.
jokes.setup: Lisa observing the dysfunction
jokes.punchline: Obscure reference to 1970s singer-songwriter as ultimate Christmas savior
jokes.explanation: Obscure pop culture reference that may land with some viewers but feels random

jokes.text: Bowl full of jelly, pump lead in his belly...
jokes.setup: Parody of 'Twas the Night Before Christmas'
jokes.punchline: Violent twist on the jolly Santa description
jokes.explanation: Decent parody but the violent humor doesn't quite fit Lisa's character

jokes.text: Some 'express.'
jokes.setup: Conductor listing all the stops on the 'Polar Express'
jokes.punchline: Bart's sarcastic observation about the many stops contradicting 'express' service
jokes.explanation: Straightforward observational humor, effective but not particularly clever

jokes.text: After NAFTA, a lot of these jobs went to the South Pole.
jokes.setup: Bart asking where all the elves are during busy season
jokes.punchline: Economic outsourcing joke applied to fantasy North Pole workshop
jokes.explanation: Clever application of real-world economics to fantasy setting, good topical humor

jokes.text: I can't lose my health insurance. My lungs are full of candy cane dust!
jokes.setup: Elf being laid off
jokes.punchline: Workplace safety concerns in whimsical Christmas setting
jokes.explanation: Good juxtaposition of serious workplace concerns with fantastical elements

jokes.text: Hey, you twos can go work for the Easter Bunny. Oh that's right. He ain't real.
jokes.setup: Elf suggesting alternative employment
jokes.punchline: Self-aware joke about fictional character denying another's existence
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor with perfect timing and awareness of the fictional hierarchy

jokes.text: Like a major airline, but you were here on time.
jokes.setup: Comparing Bart to a major airline
jokes.punchline: Subversion noting he was punctual, unlike airlines
jokes.explanation: Decent observational humor about airlines but feels like generic topical comedy

jokes.text: I didn't get anybody pregnant. I didn't Facebook a kid to death.
jokes.setup: Bart defending his 'naughty' behavior
jokes.punchline: Escalating comparison to serious modern issues
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that works for character but feels a bit forced in its contemporary references

jokes.text: It's not a sustainable business model.
jokes.setup: Santa explaining his financial troubles
jokes.punchline: Business terminology applied to Christmas magic
jokes.explanation: Good application of business logic to magical situation, fits the economic theme

jokes.text: I'm eating my own reindeer here!
jokes.setup: Santa explaining how bad things are
jokes.punchline: Shocking revelation about consuming his workforce
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation that's shocking but effective for showing Santa's desperation

jokes.text: Kids never change. Always dumb as potatoes.
jokes.setup: Bart leaving after 25 years of Earth time
jokes.punchline: Santa's cynical assessment of children's intelligence
jokes.explanation: Mildly amusing cynicism but lacks distinctive wit or surprise

jokes.text: I'm too fat to fit in a foxhole.
jokes.setup: Being questioned about why he's not in combat
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact explanation of his unfitness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic delivered with characteristic directness and self-awareness

jokes.text: Don't worry about that. I love meat and I always will.
jokes.setup: Discussion about not wanting Christmas ham
jokes.punchline: Lisa's declaration contradicting her well-established vegetarianism
jokes.explanation: Good character-based irony playing against established Lisa traits in wartime setting

jokes.text: It's round in the middle, thinning on top, and your hands get sticky when you touch it.
jokes.setup: Comparing Christmas tree to Homer
jokes.punchline: Unflattering but accurate physical description
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor with nice parallel structure and affectionate mockery

jokes.text: Well, he does, but he doesn't tell people till the last minute, so they can't make plans.
jokes.setup: Hitler doesn't take a holiday
jokes.punchline: Treating Hitler like an inconsiderate boss with poor communication
jokes.explanation: Clever humanization of historical villain through mundane workplace complaint

jokes.text: She changed her name!
jokes.setup: Reading 'Marge is MIA'
jokes.punchline: Homer misunderstanding military abbreviation as name change
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer misunderstanding that fits his character perfectly

jokes.text: Why couldn't it have been me? It still could be. You just have to go down to the recruiting station and-- Shut up.
jokes.setup: Homer lamenting it should have been him
jokes.punchline: Grandpa offering practical solution interrupted by Homer's rejection
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction showing Homer's performative grief vs. actual commitment

jokes.text: My only sale all year and it's not even a sale.
jokes.setup: Tree seller giving away the tree Marge paid for
jokes.punchline: Sad commentary on his business situation
jokes.explanation: Mildly amusing observation about economic hardship during wartime

jokes.text: Then it will cost money.
jokes.setup: Predicting TV will be invented and be free
jokes.punchline: Cynical but accurate prediction about TV's commercial evolution
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-commentary on media evolution that works both in-period and for modern viewers

jokes.text: home, family, and constantly sweeping up needles.
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining what the Christmas tree represents
jokes.punchline: Lofty ideals followed by mundane reality
jokes.explanation: Good structure contrasting ideal with practical reality

jokes.text: I pooped my tights.
jokes.setup: Maggie dressed as an angel in the nativity
jokes.punchline: Innocent bodily function disrupting solemn moment
jokes.explanation: Basic bathroom humor that works for the character but lacks sophistication

jokes.text: Nein, nein, why did I go to the 9:00 show?!
jokes.setup: Moe as German soldier in puppet show
jokes.punchline: Bilingual pun mixing German 'no' with showtime regret
jokes.explanation: Clever multilingual wordplay that works on multiple levels

jokes.text: Nein, nein, why did I go to the 9:00 show?!
jokes.setup: Nazi being attacked in theater
jokes.punchline: Complaining about show time choice while being killed
jokes.explanation: Clever bilingual pun (nein/nine) with good timing priorities

jokes.text: A picket fence stood on its end makes a sturdy and attractive ladder.
jokes.setup: How Martha got into the house
jokes.punchline: Practical home improvement advice for breaking and entering
jokes.explanation: Perfect Martha Stewart parody applying her helpful tone to criminal activity

jokes.text: Why would you want to shampoo it out?
jokes.setup: Question about washing out hair product
jokes.punchline: Martha's confusion at not wanting permanent styling
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Martha's extreme commitment to style

jokes.text: Why would you want to shampoo it out?
jokes.setup: Asking if hair product will shampoo out
jokes.punchline: Martha's horrified response suggesting permanent styling
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Martha's commitment to aesthetics over practicality

jokes.text: And finally, I wouldn't have presented it quite so proudly.
jokes.setup: Lisa showing her star made from water bottles
jokes.punchline: Martha's crushing critique disguised as helpful advice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Martha Stewart passive-aggression and perfectionism crushing child's enthusiasm

jokes.text: Lie face down and your beautiful smile will be molded into the snow.
jokes.setup: Suggesting Lisa make snow angels
jokes.punchline: Instructions that would actually be suffocating and dangerous
jokes.explanation: Dark humor disguised as helpful craft advice, fits Martha's obliviousness

jokes.text: There, a slumbering hubby is now a traditional Christmas tableau. Copyright Martha Stewart.
jokes.setup: Martha decorating sleeping Homer
jokes.punchline: Treating spouse as decoration and claiming creative ownership
jokes.explanation: Excellent Martha parody showing her commodification of everything, including family

jokes.text: It's like Christmas with a childless gay couple.
jokes.setup: Marge commenting on the perfect house
jokes.punchline: Specific demographic observation about domestic perfection
jokes.explanation: Observational humor that's mildly amusing but relies on stereotypes

jokes.text: Take an old car antenna, marinate it overnight in the tears of a heartbroken leprechaun, bake at 350 till a rainbow shoots out of the oven
jokes.setup: Explaining how to make a magic wand
jokes.punchline: Treating magical elements as ordinary craft instructions
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor applying Martha's instructional style to impossible fantasy elements

jokes.text: 'Cause they don't think this holiday's so great.
jokes.setup: Song about eating at Jewish restaurants on Christmas
jokes.punchline: Blunt explanation of why Jewish establishments are open
jokes.explanation: Simple observational humor that works but lacks sophistication

jokes.text: I wish this show had a visit from three new writers.
jokes.setup: Burns mentioning visit from three spirits
jokes.punchline: Meta-critique of the show's writing quality
jokes.explanation: Sharp meta-humor that breaks the fourth wall effectively and shows self-awareness

jokes.text: Hmm, your goblin fainted.
jokes.setup: Homer fainting after being caught
jokes.punchline: Burns' casual misidentification of Homer as a goblin
jokes.explanation: Good Burns characterization showing his detachment and poor eyesight

jokes.text: Hmm, your goblin fainted. / Not a goblin.
jokes.setup: Homer faints after neck injury revelation
jokes.punchline: Continuing elf/goblin confusion gag
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier gag, economical but not particularly strong

jokes.text: It's a Christmas miraculum.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to explain the situation
jokes.punchline: Homer's made-up word combining miracle with curriculum
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer malapropism that fits his character's tendency to mangle words

jokes.text: We, uh, blew the budget on Katy Perry.
jokes.setup: Burns threatening to release the hounds
jokes.punchline: Meta-explanation for why the hounds don't appear
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor explaining production limitations through character dialogue

jokes.text: the original, unabridged '39 Days Of Christmas'
jokes.setup: Burns wants to sing Christmas carol
jokes.punchline: Extends the traditional 12 days to absurd length
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation typical of Burns' excessive tendencies

jokes.text: Not the letter e, but the number whose exponential function is the derivative of itself.
jokes.setup: Episode brought to you by the number e
jokes.punchline: Overly technical mathematical explanation
jokes.explanation: Nerdy humor that appeals to math-savvy viewers but may alienate general audience

jokes.text: What, The Simpsons? No, Christmas.
jokes.setup: Suggesting this episode will kill something after long run
jokes.punchline: Clarifying it's Christmas, not the show itself
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that plays with audience expectations about show longevity

jokes.text: Oh, uh, that's not my belly button. / But I didn't say stop.
jokes.setup: Innocent kissing becomes inappropriate
jokes.punchline: Character encourages continuation despite awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Cheap sexual humor that relies on anatomical confusion, not particularly clever

tmdb_overview: When the rest of the family seems to lack Christmas spirit, Marge contacts Martha Clause.
